Evaluation of the LLM DeepSeek-R1 on the cybermetric_2000 benchmark using Inspect Evals

Sample input: Question: Based on the lastest advice from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), when should regular users be required to change their passwords?
Options: A) Only if the current password is compromised, B) Every 90 days, C) Every 30 days, D) Every 60 days



Model output: model='deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' choices=[ChatCompletionChoice(message=ChatMessageAssistant(id='Gd6m3GRVDYVScJKYFeFZBD', content=[ContentReasoning(type='reasoning', reasoning='Okay, so I need to figure out when regular users should change their passwords according to the latest NIST guidelines. Hmm, I remember that NIST updated their recommendations a few years back. Let me think.\n\nWait, the old advice was like changing passwords every 90 days or something. But I heard that they changed that. Oh right! The newer guidelines from NIST (maybe SP 800-63B?) say that frequent password changes aren\'t necessary unless there\'s a known compromise. Because forcing regular changes can lead to users picking weaker passwords, like incrementing a number each time. So the best practice now is to only require a password change if there\'s evidence it\'s been compromised. \n\nLooking at the options, option A says "Only if the current password is compromised." That matches what I remember. The other options (B, C, D) are all time-based, which NIST now advises against. So the correct answer should be A.', signature=None, redacted=False), ContentText(type='text', text='ANSWER: A', refusal=None)], source='generate', internal=None, role='assistant', tool_calls=None, model='deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1'), stop_reason='stop', logprobs=None)] usage=ModelUsage(input_tokens=132, output_tokens=204, total_tokens=336, input_tokens_cache_write=None, input_tokens_cache_read=None, reasoning_tokens=None) time=3.670978643000126 metadata=None error=None
